question.text: Under Section 11A(1), what can SEBI specify by regulation regarding companies?

answer.text: Matters relating to issue of capital, transfer of securities, and the manner of disclosure by companies
The maximum share price companies can charge in IPOs
The minimum dividend companies must declare
The salary structure of company directors

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 11A(1) empowers SEBI to specify by regulations: (i) matters relating to issue of capital, transfer of securities, and incidental matters, and (ii) the manner in which such matters shall be disclosed by companies. **The Trap:** SEBI doesn't set share prices, mandate dividends, or regulate director salaries. Its power under 11A relates to capital issuance procedures and disclosure requirements. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Section 11A is the foundation for SEBI's ICDR (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations — governing IPOs, rights issues, and public offerings.
